title: Power BI - Power Query M Language Reference
description: M language syntax, SharePoint functions, CSV parsing, table/text/date functions, complete templates

m_syntax_basics.step_references: Each step name refers to the result of that step. Steps are NOT sequential instructions — they are named expressions. Power Query only evaluates steps reachable from the 'in' expression.

type_system.currency: Currency.Type — fixed decimal with 4 decimal places
type_system.date: type date — date without time, e.g. #date(2024, 1, 15)
type_system.datetime: type datetime — date with time, e.g. #datetime(2024, 1, 15, 9, 30, 0)
type_system.datetimezone: type datetimezone — datetime with timezone offset
type_system.duration: type duration — time span, e.g. #duration(1, 0, 0, 0) = 1 day
type_system.record: type record — name-value pairs (like a row), e.g. [Name="Alice", Age=30]
type_system.binary: type binary — raw bytes (file content, images)
type_system.null: null — absence of a value (equivalent to BLANK in DAX)

sharepoint_files.signature: SharePoint.Files(url as text, [options as record]) as table
sharepoint_files.description: Connects to a SharePoint site and returns a table listing ALL files in ALL document libraries on that site, recursively.
sharepoint_files.url_format: Root site URL only, e.g. https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MySite. Do NOT include the library or folder path.
sharepoint_files.options: [ApiVersion=15] for SharePoint Online. 15 = REST API v15.
sharepoint_files.notes: Returns metadata for all files. The Content column holds the binary file data needed for parsing.

sharepoint_contents.signature: SharePoint.Contents(url as text, [options as record]) as table
sharepoint_contents.description: Returns a hierarchical folder/file structure rather than a flat file list. Useful for navigating to a specific library first.
sharepoint_contents.example:
// Navigate to a specific library
Source = SharePoint.Contents("https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MySite", [ApiVersion=15]),
DocLib = Source{[Name="Shared Documents"]}[Content],
SubFolder = DocLib{[Name="Reports"]}[Content]

csv_document_function.signature: Csv.Document(source as any, [columns as any], [delimiter as any], [extraValues as any], [encoding as any]) as table
csv_document_function.description: Parses a CSV binary into a table. The record form is preferred for explicit parameter naming.
csv_document_function.record_form_example:
Csv.Document(
    binaryContent,
    [
        Delimiter    = ",",          // column separator
        Columns      = null,          // null = auto-detect column count
        Encoding     = 65001,         // 65001 = UTF-8; 1252 = Windows Latin-1
        QuoteStyle   = QuoteStyle.None // or QuoteStyle.Csv (handles quoted fields with commas)
    ]
)

SelectRows.signature: Table.SelectRows(table as table, condition as function) as table
SelectRows.description: Filter rows keeping only those where condition returns true

AddColumn.signature: Table.AddColumn(table, newColumnName as text, columnGenerator as function, [columnType as type]) as table

TransformColumnTypes.signature: Table.TransformColumnTypes(table, typeTransformations as list, [culture as nullable text]) as table
TransformColumnTypes.description: Cast columns to specified types in one step

RenameColumns.signature: Table.RenameColumns(table, renames as list) as table

SelectColumns.signature: Table.SelectColumns(table, columns as list) as table
SelectColumns.description: Keep only the specified columns (in the specified order)

RemoveColumns.signature: Table.RemoveColumns(table, columns as list) as table

PromoteHeaders.description: Make the first row the column headers

Combine.description: Union / append multiple tables into one. All tables should have compatible schemas.

ExpandTableColumn.signature: Table.ExpandTableColumn(table, column as text, columnNames as list, [newColumnNames as list]) as table
ExpandTableColumn.description: Expand a column containing nested tables into multiple flat columns

Group.signature: Table.Group(table, key as list, aggregatedColumns as list) as table
Group.description: Group rows by key columns and apply aggregation functions

Sort.signature: Table.Sort(table, comparisonCriteria as list) as table

Skip.signature: Table.Skip(table, countOrCondition as any) as table

FirstN.description: Keep only the first N rows

Pivot.signature: Table.Pivot(table, pivotValues, attributeColumn, valueColumn, [aggregation]) as table
Pivot.description: Pivot a column of values into multiple columns
Pivot.example:
// Turn month names into columns
Table.Pivot(Source, List.Distinct(Source[MonthName]), "MonthName", "Amount", List.Sum)

Unpivot.signature: Table.Unpivot(table, pivotColumns, attributeNewColumnName, valueNewColumnName) as table
Unpivot.description: Unpivot multiple columns into rows (opposite of pivot)
Unpivot.example:
// Turn month columns Jan, Feb, Mar into rows
Table.Unpivot(Source, {"Jan", "Feb", "Mar"}, "Month", "Amount")

Join.signature: Table.Join(table1, key1, table2, key2, [joinKind]) as table
Join.description: Join two tables. JoinKind options: JoinKind.Inner (default), Left, Right, Full, LeftAnti, RightAnti

TransformColumns.signature: Table.TransformColumns(table, transformations as list) as table
TransformColumns.description: Apply a transformation function to values in specified columns

text_functions.PadEnd:
Text.PadEnd(text, count, [paddingCharacter])
// Pad with character on the right

date_functions.FromText:
Date.FromText(text, [options])
// Parse text as date. Example: Date.FromText("2024-01-15")
date_functions.DateTimeZone_UtcNow:
DateTimeZone.UtcNow() as datetimezone
// Current UTC datetime with timezone offset

list_functions.Select:
List.Select(list, condition)
// Filter list: List.Select({1,2,3,4}, each _ > 2)  // {3, 4}
list_functions.Transform:
List.Transform(list, transform)
// Apply function to each: List.Transform({1,2,3}, each _ * 2)  // {2, 4, 6}

record_functions.FieldNames:
Record.FieldNames(record) as list
// Returns list of field names
record_functions.FieldValues:
Record.FieldValues(record) as list
// Returns list of field values
record_functions.HasFields:
Record.HasFields(record, fields)
// Check if record has specified field(s)
record_functions.FromList:
Record.FromList(list, fields)
// Create record from parallel lists of values and field names

complete_monthly_template.description: Complete production-ready M query for combining monthly CSVs from SharePoint. Handles UTF-8 encoding, extracts year/month from filename, casts types, and builds a MonthDate column.

custom_function_pattern.description: Define a reusable M function query that transforms a single CSV binary. The combining query calls this function for each file row.
custom_function_pattern.function_definition:
// Query named: fnParseMonthlyCSV
// This function takes one CSV binary and one filename, returns a table
(csvBinary as binary, fileName as text) as table =>
let
    Raw      = Csv.Document(csvBinary, [Delimiter=",", Encoding=65001, QuoteStyle=QuoteStyle.Csv]),
    Headers  = Table.PromoteHeaders(Raw, [PromoteAllScalars=true]),
    AddFile  = Table.AddColumn(Headers, "SourceFile",  each fileName,                               type text),
    AddYear  = Table.AddColumn(AddFile,  "FileYear",   each Number.FromText(Text.Middle(fileName, 7, 4)), Int64.Type),
    AddMonth = Table.AddColumn(AddYear,  "FileMonth",  each Number.FromText(Text.Middle(fileName, 12, 2)), Int64.Type),
    AddDate  = Table.AddColumn(AddMonth, "MonthDate",  each #date([FileYear], [FileMonth], 1),       type date)
in
    AddDate
custom_function_pattern.main_query_using_function:
// Main combining query — calls fnParseMonthlyCSV for each file
let
    Source    = SharePoint.Files("https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MySite", [ApiVersion=15]),
    Filtered  = Table.SelectRows(Source, each Text.Contains([Folder Path], "/Reports/") and [Extension] = ".csv"),
    Invoked   = Table.AddColumn(Filtered, "_Parsed", each fnParseMonthlyCSV([Content], [Name])),
    Expanded  = Table.Combine(Invoked[_Parsed])
in
    Expanded
